name: Donna Brazile
subject: This isn't the first time Trump has attacked our democracy
body:
Protect our democracy
---
**Add your name: say you're committed to protecting our democracy.

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Protect-Our-Democracy1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170707_db_v1s3&source=em_20170707_db_v1s3")**
Derek --

Every time I think Republicans have sunk as low as they can, they manage to find a way to go lower.

Low was Donald Trump pretending that the reason he lost the popular vote was because as many as 5 million Americans "illegally" voted.

Lower was when he established a commission to investigate "voter fraud" -- a wholly made-up problem invented by Republicans as a pretext to make it more difficult for folks to vote.

And last week, we hit rock bottom when Kris Kobach -- the man behind some of this country's most severe voting restrictions, hand-picked by Trump to run this new sham of a commission -- demanded that each state hand over its voter rolls.

Handing over all your personal information, including your political party and voting history, to Donald Trump? I don't think so. Thank God almost every state has refused this absurd request.

But here's the simple truth: this isn't the first time Donald Trump and his cronies have tried to undermine our democracy, and it won't be the last.

We have to keep pushing back on Trump until this ridiculous commission is disbanded for good, and do everything in our power to protect our right to vote.

**There's no fight more important than this, and I'm ready for it. If you are too, add your name right now and let me know:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Protect-Our-Democracy1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170707_db_v1s3&source=em_20170707_db_v1s3")**
Thanks,

Donna

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052325c/4067b0a/36976c0c8/7e6a0784/1591034560/VEsF/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20170707_db_v1s3&source=em_20170707_db_v1s3"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052325c/4067b0a/36976c0c8/7e6a0785/1591034560/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170707_db_v1s3&source=em_20170707_db_v1s3"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052325c/4067b0a/36976c0c8/7e6a0782/1591034560/VEsD/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170707_db_v1s3&source=em_20170707_db_v1s3") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052325c/4067b0a/36976c0c8/7e6a0783/1591034560/VEsA/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170707_db_v1s3&source=em_20170707_db_v1s3"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: Trump declares war on voters
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

President Trump and Vice-President Pence’s “election integrity”
commission is unequivocally declaring war on voters -- and right now,
two prominent Democrats are helping.

The commission recently sent a letter to all 50 states requesting the
very same data that Republican state governments have used to create
hyper-partisan gerrymandering and enact restrictive voter ID laws
across the country.

This commission is laying the groundwork for nationalized voter
suppression. It is completely illegitimate and will harm our
elections.

But the presence of the secretaries of state for Maine and New
Hampshire on this commission -- both prominent Democrats -- is making
it look bipartisan. And that gives it a greater chance of succeeding.
We can’t allow that to happen.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/votercommission_dems?t=1002&akid=131.356062.1EZ9p8 ]Sign LegitAction’s petition to the Maine and New Hampshire
Democrats on the Trump voter suppression commission: Resign from this
illegitimate voter suppression effort.

Nationally, the Democratic Party is gaining support as the country’s
demographics become increasingly diverse. The Republican Party has
known for several years that its best tactic to cling to power is not
to build a party worth supporting, but to deny participation in the
political process to Democratic voters.

So Republicans have been suppressing voters state-by-state for years.
And now that they control the White House, they’re trying to do it
nationally.

By agreeing to participate in this commission, Maine Secretary of
State Matthew Dunlap (D) and New Hampshire Secretary of State Bill
Gardner (D) are helping.

But don’t take my word for it. National voter suppression expert and
co-chair of the commission Kris Kobach even went on CNN this week to
tout the commission’s bipartisan credentials. We have to make it clear
this commission doesn’t have legitimate support from both parties.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/votercommission_dems?t=1003&akid=131.356062.1EZ9p8 ]Tell Democrats participating in Trump’s voter suppression
commission: Resign from this illegitimate voter suppression effort.
Deny Trump the sheen of bipartisanship.

When people are denied participation in the political process, it
undermines the legitimacy of our elections -- and ultimately
undermines the legitimacy of our government itself. We must not let
President Trump suppress legitimate votes.

Thank you for standing up for our democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/votercommission_dems?t=1004&akid=131.356062.1EZ9p8 ]TAKE ACTION »

name: MaggieHassan.com
subject: what's your health care story, everyone?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdef6/2a380d31/19ae3cf2/fe4ae0/3380181190/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdef6/2a380d31/19ae3cf2/fe4ae0/3380181190/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
Hello --
The fight to protect affordable health care and Medicaid is a personal one for Senator Hassan.
Her son Ben experiences severe disabilities covered in part by Medicaid, and she believes that every family deserves access to the same support that her family has had.
But the Hassan family's story isn't unique -- millions of families across the country have had their lives improved because of access to health care. Is yours one of them?
**If so, we'd love to hear from you. It's so important for stories like yours to be heard. Your bravery is the best way for us to show Washington Republicans that their recklessness and heartlessness hurts real people and real families.
Thank you for sharing, everyone. Your story is a critical part in protecting access to health care and preventing cruel, damaging bills like Trumpcare from ever being signed into law.
-- Team Maggie

name: Tim Kaine
subject: only Congress can declare war
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

Last week, a House panel adopted a measure to repeal the 2001 Authorization for Use of Military Force, which President Trump has used to justify carrying out military actions in foreign countries without going before or even presenting a plan to Congress.

It's clear now that, at a time when tensions are high across the globe, both Democrats and Republicans agree we should rein in the President's ability to take our country to war without congressional approval.

I've said this before and I'll say it again: **No president should be able to take our country into a war without authorization from Congress. That 's what it says in the Constitution, and it's a principle I stand for.**

That's why Senator Jeff Flake and I have introduced a bipartisan resolution to authorize our current military operations against Isis, Al Qaeda and the Taliban with strict limitations on the duration and geographic expanse of the effort. No President should have the ability to wage war without a congressional vote.

I know it's important to our troops, especially the tens of thousands of servicemembers from Virginia, to know the country is behind them, and important to our allies who want to stand with us.

**[Will you sign my petition to demand Congress exercise its Constitutional duty and vote on whether to authorize military force?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=DWG8Nj48bYkUx9fNri3AqL8KA%2FUqRjc4)**

I'll say now what I said under President Obama's Administration: By not taking an active role in authorizing military force, Congress is abdicating its responsibility to those we represent -- we must act as a check on the President.

Thank you,

Tim

name: Tom Perez
subject: I know garbage when I see it, Derek
body:
Stand with Democrats to protect affordable health care.
---
**Add your name if you're still committed to fighting back against the GOP's health care repeal.

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stay-Committed1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170707_tp_nd&source=em_20170707_tp_nd")**
Derek, I used to work on a trash truck, so I know garbage when I see it.

The GOP's health care repeal bill is a flaming dumpster fire.

If it becomes law, 22 million people will lose their health coverage and $800 billion will be slashed from Medicaid, all to pay for tax breaks for millionaires and billionaires.

That's not an exaggeration, Derek -- Republicans literally want to steal from the sick to give to the rich.

Here's some good news, though: what you're doing is working. You're rallying, calling, protesting, and petitioning -- and you've given the GOP pause. Mitch McConnell delayed the repeal vote last week because of you.

But the GOP hasn't given up. After seven years of clamoring to repeal the Affordable Care Act, you can be certain they're not going to go down without a fight.

So let's give them one. Let's keep rallying and calling, protesting and petitioning -- we have to keep making our voices heard until this repeal bill is dead for good.

**I need to know you're still in this, Derek, because we can only win this fight if we're all in it together. If you are, say so today:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "https://my.democrats.org/Stay-Committed1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170707_tp_nd&source=em_20170707_tp_nd")**
Thanks,

Tom

**_Save the Dates_**
On behalf of our team here at __**your**__ Indiana Republican Party, I hope you had a great Fourth of July celebrating the 241st anniversary of our Independence. I know this time of year comes with a lot of demands on your time, so with Summer heating up (literally), I wanted to highlight some of the great events we have coming up that I think will be a lot of fun. If we've proven anything in the past few months, it's that we know how to throw a good Party!

**[July 18](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=390ff65b81&e=1acd9bb3d6)** \- Governor Holcomb's Sporting Clay Shoot with First Lady Janet Holcomb & Colts Kicker Adam Vinatieri
**[August 4-20](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4943af87cd&e=1acd9bb3d6)** \- Republican Booth at the Indiana State Fair (contact **James Cecil** at jcecil@indiana.gop if you're interested in volunteering at the booth. We'll be there 12 hours a day, every day!)
**August 8** \- Young Professionals for Team Holcomb with two **__very__** special guests to be announced soon
**Fall 2017** \- Congress of Counties (more information to come soon!)

Remember, you can always see a listing of statewide events being held by our county party organizations in the "Events" section below.
\- Kyle

(Photo: Chairman Hupfer speaks to 600 Hoosier Republicans at the Team Holcomb Launch Party at the Lucas Estate on Tuesday, June 27, 2017.)
|
---
| | _**Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**_
---
| |
---
Governor **Eric Holcomb** kicked off the July Fourth Holiday by celebrating his wedding anniversary with First Lady **Janet Holcomb**! Be sure to mark July 2nd on your calendar so you can remember to drop them a note next year! The two also participated in the Brownsburg Fourth of July Parade.

On Wednesday, the Governor made a major announcement alongside several other state officials regarding our state's fight against the drug epidemic in underserved areas. Allen, Johnson, Monroe, Tippecanoe and Vigo counties will now be home to opioid treatment centers. As FOX 59 reported, "the five sites were strategically selected so that they're based in or near counties where use of naloxone, the life-saving drug that can reserve the effects of an overdose, is high." You can read more about the announcement [here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=23f5100c3e&e=1acd9bb3d6).

| _**In Case You Missed It: We're Going to Evansville!**_
---
| In case you missed the big news in the most recent edition of The Party Line, we're headed to Evansville next year for the 2018 Indiana Republican Party State Convention. Mayor Lloyd Winnecke assembled an outstanding Host Committee that blew us away with their proposal for two days of Republican fun in Southwest Indiana. Stay tuned for more information as we approach our biennial gathering.

|

| _**State Senator Luke Kenley to Retire**_
State Senator Luke Kenley announced this week that he will retire effective September 30, 2017. Read more about his role in crafting Indiana's balanced budgets [here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=96802aa94d&e=1acd9bb3d6). The Indiana Republican Party will announce a date for a caucus to replace Sen. Kenley soon.

body:
28 Senate offices in 21 states demanding Senators vote no on the GOP health care plan.
#
Friends,
We're still sitting in Ohio Sen. Rob Portman's office right now as part of yesterday's nationwide day of action against the morally repugnant Republican health care plan. Some of us participated in actions at Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell's office yesterday afternoon as well, and have spent more than 24 hours protesting.
Yesterday, Our Revolution local groups like ours, along with partners at the Working Families Party, Democratic Socialists of America, Ultraviolet, Progressive Democrats of America, #AllOfUs, Democracy Spring, ResistHere.org, and the People's Consortium for Human and Civil Rights sat in 28 Senate offices in 21 states demanding Senators vote no on the GOP health care plan. Across the country, nearly 50 people were arrested for speaking out against draconian cuts to Medicaid in order to give billions of dollars in tax breaks to the wealthiest Americans.
[](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/take-action-to-protect-health-care?source=em170707-full "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/take-action-to-protect-health-care?source=em170707-full")
As a result of our collective efforts, in person and through phone calls, Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell admitted he's having trouble getting the votes to pass their plan. Thanks to all of your efforts we are winning the fight to save health care for millions of people!
Our Revolution groups from Alaska to Florida, to Texas and Maine all came out to tell their Senator that health care is a human right. Groups in Columbus, Ohio and Tallahassee, Florida are also still sitting in Senate offices. These actions showed we are truly powerful when we work together.
**[We're close, but we can't stop fighting yet. We're going to flood the Senate's phone lines when they return from recess next week--help keep up the pressure by committing to call your Senator.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/take-action-to-protect-health-care?source=em170707-full "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/take-action-to-protect-health-care?source=em170707-full")**
The will of the people is on our side. With more actions like the ones we took yesterday, we can defeat the Republican health care repeal and fight for the real solution that will give health care coverage to everyone, Medicare for All.
In solidarity,
Our Revolution Greater Cincinnati
**[I WILL DEFEND HEALTH CARE»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/take-action-to-protect-health-care?source=em170707-full "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/take-action-to-protect-health-care?source=em170707-full")**

**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, July 7th |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,
With the fiscally responsible bi-partisan budget behind us, the NHGOP is focusing on the many special elections coming up. With staff and volunteers knocking on doors, making phone calls, and arranging events, we expect positive results. Aside from that, we continue to make sure we keep our Democratic counterparts honest.
In the last few days, that's been quite a task!
Ten years ago the NH Democratic Party had no issue with selling detailed voter information to Democratic campaigns. We should actually be amazed that they were embracing the tenets of capitalism. They "improved" upon the product they purchased from the Secretary of State by expanding the data so much that five Democratic campaigns paid $325k combined for the voter files! Now, the same Democrats who profited from selling voter data are complaining that sharing this publicly available data is a violation of trust.
Friends, there are plenty of legitimate debates and partisan disagreements that Democrats can engage Republicans with, but they seem to be avoiding those in lieu of hyper-partisan attacks via social media with no merit.
The Democratic Party is keeping up its obstructionist behavior to resist our President and our Governor, even going the extra mile by attacking NH SOS Bill Gardner, a lifelong Democrat. While they are attacking Secretary Gardner, the NHDP has yet to comment on former DNC Chair Howard Dean's comments that our status as the First In the Nation should be challenged.
It is sad and pathetic that rather than working with Republicans to ensure a basic shared tenet like voter integrity, they continue to act in ways that are not in the best interests of New Hampshire and its citizens.
Today, won't you follow us on Twitter and Facebook and help push back against their hypocrisy?
Sincerely, _Jeanie_

clean_body: | | | NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, July 7th | ---|---|--- | | A Note From Jeanie | | | Dear Derek, With the fiscally responsible bi-partisan budget behind us, the NHGOP is focusing on the many special elections coming up. With staff and volunteers knocking on doors, making phone calls, and arranging events, we expect positive results. Aside from that, we continue to make sure we keep our Democratic counterparts honest. In the last few days, that's been quite a task! Ten years ago the NH Democratic Party had no issue with selling detailed voter information to Democratic campaigns. We should actually be amazed that they were embracing the tenets of capitalism. They "improved" upon the product they purchased from the Secretary of State by expanding the data so much that five Democratic campaigns paid $325k combined for the voter files! Now, the same Democrats who profited from selling voter data are complaining that sharing this publicly available data is a violation of trust. Friends, there are plenty of legitimate debates and partisan disagreements that Democrats can engage Republicans with, but they seem to be avoiding those in lieu of hyper-partisan attacks via social media with no merit. The Democratic Party is keeping up its obstructionist behavior to resist our President and our Governor, even going the extra mile by attacking NH SOS Bill Gardner, a lifelong Democrat. While they are attacking Secretary Gardner, the NHDP has yet to comment on former DNC Chair Howard Dean's comments that our status as the First In the Nation should be challenged. It is sad and pathetic that rather than working with Republicans to ensure a basic shared tenet like voter integrity, they continue to act in ways that are not in the best interests of New Hampshire and its citizens. Today, won't you follow us on Twitter and Facebook and help push back against their hypocrisy?
